test_procedure: Inspect the latest user-role export, access review worksheet, exception list, and approval record. Select a sample of privileged users and verify whether access was reviewed and signed off within the quarter.
finding: User access review is defined conceptually, but there is no consistent quarterly sign-off record for privileged access review.
recommendation: Formalize quarterly user access review with reviewer sign-off, exception tracking, removal evidence, and management approval.
source_reference: NIST SP 800-53A assessment approach; NIST SP 800-53 AC/IA; NIST CSF PR.AA/GV.RR; CIS Account Management; UU PDP
notes: This assessment supports access governance and personal data protection readiness.

test_procedure: Review access request forms, approval records, user lifecycle reconciliation, and termination samples. Verify that sensitive access is approved before provisioning and removed after role change or offboarding.
finding: Access approval evidence exists for some requests, but lifecycle reconciliation and removal evidence are incomplete.
recommendation: Implement a monthly joiner-mover-leaver reconciliation and require access removal evidence for role changes and terminations.
source_reference: NIST SP 800-53A assessment approach; NIST SP 800-53 AC/IA/PS; NIST CSF PR.AA/PR.DS; CIS Account Management; UU PDP
notes: Maps to both data privacy and user lifecycle risk.

test_procedure: Inspect admin activity log extracts, monthly review checklist, and escalation notes. Verify whether unusual privileged activity is reviewed and exceptions are escalated.
finding: System logs are assumed to exist, but there is no documented monthly admin activity log review or exception escalation evidence.
recommendation: Define a monthly admin log review procedure, exception criteria, reviewer responsibility, and escalation workflow.
source_reference: NIST SP 800-53A assessment approach; NIST SP 800-53 AU; NIST CSF DE.CM/GV.OV; CIS Audit Log Management
notes: Weak logging review increases detection and accountability risk.

test_procedure: Inspect backup schedule, restoration test log, test screenshot, result validation, and management sign-off. Verify whether restoration testing was performed for critical data before peak academic periods.
finding: Backup activity is assumed, but there is no documented restoration test evidence to confirm recovery readiness.
recommendation: Perform quarterly backup restoration testing and document test scope, result, issues, remediation, and sign-off.
source_reference: NIST SP 800-53A assessment approach; NIST SP 800-53 CP; NIST CSF RC.RP/PR.DS; CIS Data Recovery; ISO 27001 availability concept
notes: High-priority availability and continuity remediation candidate.

test_procedure: Review a sample of change requests, approvals, UAT results, rollback notes, and implementation records. Verify whether changes were approved and tested before release.
finding: Change approval exists for some changes, but UAT evidence and rollback documentation are inconsistent.
recommendation: Standardize change request documentation, UAT sign-off, rollback plan, and post-implementation review before production release.
source_reference: NIST SP 800-53A assessment approach; NIST SP 800-53 CM/SA; NIST CSF PR.PS/GV.OV; COBIT BAI06 concept

test_procedure: Inspect incident tickets, severity classification, escalation timestamps, closure notes, and post-incident review records. Verify whether incidents are handled consistently.
finding: Incident tickets are available, but severity classification and escalation evidence are not consistently documented.
recommendation: Define an incident severity matrix, escalation SLA, required ticket fields, and post-incident review checklist.
source_reference: NIST SP 800-53A assessment approach; NIST SP 800-53 IR; NIST CSF RS.MA/RS.CO/RS.AN; CIS Incident Response Management

test_procedure: Inspect vendor SLA document, support contact list, escalation procedure, and periodic vendor review notes. Verify whether vendor support expectations and recovery responsibilities are documented.
finding: Vendor dependency is recognized, but SLA review and escalation testing are not consistently documented.
recommendation: Maintain vendor SLA review records, escalation contacts, support scope, and periodic dependency review notes.
source_reference: NIST SP 800-53A assessment approach; NIST SP 800-53 SR; NIST CSF GV.SC/GV.OV; CIS Service Provider Management; COBIT vendor governance concept

test_procedure: Inspect retention schedule, deletion review worksheet, policy approval, and sample data lifecycle evidence. Verify whether personal data retention and deletion are governed.
finding: No formal retention schedule or deletion review evidence is available for student-related personal data.
recommendation: Define data retention rules, deletion review process, data owner approval, and evidence documentation for personal data lifecycle governance.
source_reference: NIST SP 800-53A assessment approach; NIST SP 800-53 PT/MP; NIST CSF PR.DS/GV.PO; UU PDP; ISO 27001 confidentiality concept
notes: High-priority privacy and compliance remediation candidate.

test_procedure: Inspect training materials, completion records, onboarding checklist, and awareness communication. Verify whether admin users complete relevant security and privacy awareness activities.
finding: Training may exist informally, but completion records and role-specific privacy/security awareness evidence are incomplete.
recommendation: Create annual awareness training completion tracking and onboarding awareness checklist for admin users handling sensitive data.
source_reference: NIST SP 800-53A assessment approach; NIST SP 800-53 AT; NIST CSF PR.AT/GV.RR; CIS Security Awareness and Skills Training

test_procedure: Review reporting validation checklist, reconciliation output, exception log, and reviewer sign-off. Verify whether reporting data quality checks are performed and documented.
finding: Data validation is performed in some cases, but reconciliation evidence and exception resolution tracking are not standardized.
recommendation: Standardize monthly reporting validation checklist, reconciliation steps, exception owner, and reviewer sign-off.
source_reference: NIST SP 800-53A assessment approach; NIST SP 800-53 SI; NIST CSF ID.IM/PR.DS; ISO 27001 integrity concept
notes: Maps to reporting and data integrity risks.

test_procedure: Inspect continuity checklist, peak readiness review, simulation notes, fallback procedure, and owner confirmation. Verify readiness for course registration or other critical academic periods.
finding: Continuity need is recognized, but walkthrough/testing evidence and fallback ownership are not fully documented.
recommendation: Document continuity procedures, perform pre-peak readiness walkthrough, and assign fallback owners for critical academic services.
source_reference: NIST SP 800-53A assessment approach; NIST SP 800-53 CP; NIST CSF RC.RP/GV.RM; CIS Data Recovery; COBIT continuity concept

test_procedure: Inspect document request records, approval notes, fulfillment evidence, and sample QA review results. Verify whether closure is supported by traceable evidence.
finding: Workflow records exist, but closure evidence and sample review results are not consistently linked to each request.
recommendation: Require closure evidence, approval note, reviewer field, and monthly sample review for document request fulfillment.
source_reference: NIST SP 800-53A assessment approach; NIST SP 800-53 PL/CM; NIST CSF GV.OV/PR.PS; COBIT monitoring concept
notes: Strengthens evidence trail and audit readiness.

test_procedure: Inspect vulnerability backlog, severity rating, asset owner assignment, remediation due date, and closure evidence. Verify whether known vulnerabilities are tracked and prioritized.
finding: No formal vulnerability backlog or patch tracking evidence is available for in-scope assets.
recommendation: Create a vulnerability backlog linked to asset inventory, severity, owner, due date, remediation status, and closure evidence.
source_reference: NIST SP 800-53A assessment approach; NIST SP 800-53 RA/SI; NIST CSF ID.RA/PR.PS/DE.CM; CIS Continuous Vulnerability Management
notes: Optional NVD vulnerability sample can be added later.

test_procedure: Inspect personal data export request log, approval records, data scope notes, purpose justification, and monthly export review. Verify whether bulk exports are governed.
finding: Personal data export review control is planned but not yet implemented in the simulated control environment.
recommendation: Implement approval and log review for bulk personal data exports, including purpose, scope, requester, approver, and retention note.
source_reference: NIST SP 800-53A assessment approach; NIST SP 800-53 AC/AU/PT; NIST CSF GV.PO/PR.DS/DE.CM; UU PDP
notes: Enhancement control for privacy compliance and accountability.

test_procedure: Inspect evidence tracker, repository index, reviewer notes, and control owner updates. Verify whether evidence status is centralized and reviewed periodically.
finding: Centralized control evidence repository is planned but not yet implemented.
recommendation: Build an evidence tracker and repository index to centralize evidence ownership, review status, and management reporting.
source_reference: NIST SP 800-53A assessment approach; NIST SP 800-53 CA/PM; NIST CSF GV.OV/GV.RM/ID.IM; COBIT monitoring and assurance concept
notes: Will connect directly to evidence tracker and management summary.
